title: Avatar: Fire and Ash Box Office Performance
text: Avatar: Fire and Ash held the top position with $64 million during the December 26-28 weekend, representing a modest 28% decline from its opening week. After just 10 days in theaters, the film accumulated $217.6 million domestically. The article notes that this is the seventh best of all time for December, positioning it ahead of the original Avatar's comparable period performance. The franchise continues its dominance internationally. The film has reached $760 million worldwide, and notably, the trilogy is the first to gross $6 billion worldwide, according to the report. Looking ahead, box office analysts anticipate the film could potentially exceed $600 million domestically if it follows traditional Avatar patterns, though the exact trajectory remains dependent on upcoming weekend performance. The article suggests that a billion dollars is coming soon for the global total. This third installment demonstrates continued audience confidence in James Cameron's vision, with the holiday timing providing steady viewership momentum through the December theatrical window.
